speaker: YORK
text: Of Salisbury, who can report of him,

speaker: YORK
text: That winter lion, who in rage forgets

speaker: YORK
text: Agèd contusions and all brush of time,

speaker: YORK
text: And, like a gallant in the brow of youth,

speaker: YORK
text: Repairs him with occasion? This happy day

speaker: YORK
text: Is not itself, nor have we won one foot,

speaker: YORK
text: If Salisbury be lost.

speaker: RICHARD
text: My noble father,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Three times today I holp him to his horse,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Three times bestrid him. Thrice I led him off,

speaker: RICHARD
text: Persuaded him from any further act;

speaker: RICHARD
text: But still, where danger was, still there I met him,

speaker: RICHARD
text: And, like rich hangings in a homely house,

speaker: RICHARD
text: So was his will in his old feeble body.

speaker: RICHARD
text: But, noble as he is, look where he comes.

speaker: RICHARD
text: Now, by my sword, well hast thou fought today!

speaker: SALISBURY
text: By th’ Mass, so did we all. I thank you, Richard.

speaker: SALISBURY
text: God knows how long it is I have to live,

speaker: SALISBURY
text: And it hath pleased Him that three times today

speaker: SALISBURY
text: You have defended me from imminent death.

speaker: SALISBURY
text: Well, lords, we have not got that which we have;

speaker: SALISBURY
text: ’Tis not enough our foes are this time fled,

speaker: SALISBURY
text: Being opposites of such repairing nature.

speaker: YORK
text: I know our safety is to follow them;

speaker: YORK
text: For, as I hear, the King is fled to London

speaker: YORK
text: To call a present court of Parliament.

speaker: YORK
text: Let us pursue him ere the writs go forth.—

speaker: YORK
text: What says Lord Warwick? Shall we after them?

speaker: WARWICK
text: After them? Nay, before them, if we can.

speaker: WARWICK
text: Now, by my hand, lords, ’twas a glorious day.

speaker: WARWICK
text: Saint Albans battle won by famous York

speaker: WARWICK
text: Shall be eternized in all age to come.—

speaker: WARWICK
text: Sound drum and trumpets, and to London all;

speaker: WARWICK
text: And more such days as these to us befall!
